objectives.title: A clear, bounded vision & declared intent

checklist.label: Define the primary purpose and land use type for this project
Define 3-5 measurable success criteria for the first planning cycle
Confirm the primary steward and any co-stewards for this project
Identify non-negotiables and hard constraints
Classify vision elements as committed vs. aspirational
Record assumptions and known unknowns

objectives.title: A constituted steward team & capability register

checklist.label: List all people contributing to this project - resident and non-resident, primary and supporting
Define the role of each person - what they are responsible for on this land
Assign decision rights by domain - who has authority over what
Inventory capabilities by domain for each contributor - what skills each person brings
Record available labour by person - hours per week and seasonal variation across the year
Record available capital - initial budget, annual operating budget, and funding sources
Identify skill gaps and resolution priorities - training, hire, or contract
Note the governance principles and decision-making framework that will govern the team
Map property boundaries on base layer
Obtain and verify current title and deed documents
Identify all easements, rights of way, and encumbrances
Check zoning and permitted land uses
Identify water rights and entitlements
Record covenant, heritage, or conservation obligations
Note required permits for planned activities - building, earthworks, water harvesting

objectives.title: A mapped picture of stakeholders & community

checklist.label: Map immediate neighbours and shared boundary relationships
Identify local authority contacts and relevant officers
Record any Indigenous land relationships or cultural obligations
Identify community members affected by or interested in the project
Note existing conflict, goodwill, or partnership relationships
Record preferred communication channels for each stakeholder group
List all household food needs by category - vegetables, fruit, protein, dairy, staples
Estimate current annual spend per category as a baseline
Define self-sufficiency targets per category - full, partial, or not applicable
Identify non-food household needs the land could supply - fuel, fibre, medicine, building materials
Define minimum provision threshold - the baseline that makes the homestead viable
Record which needs will always be sourced externally and why
Confirm household agreement on priorities and trade-offs

objectives.title: A clear read of terrain & topography

checklist.label: Produce or obtain topographic map with contour intervals appropriate to scale
Identify slope gradients and aspects across the site
Map elevation range and drainage divides
Identify flat areas, ridgelines, saddles, and hollows
Note areas of instability, erosion, or landslip risk

objectives.title: A clear read of climate & sectors

checklist.label: Record annual and seasonal rainfall averages and variability
Map prevailing wind directions by season
Record temperature range, frost dates, and heat event frequency
Map sun angles and shade zones by season
Identify fire risk sectors and direction
Note microclimate variations across the site

objectives.title: A clear read of existing ecology & habitat

checklist.label: Record existing vegetation communities by zone
Identify native and invasive species present
Note wildlife corridors, nesting sites, and movement patterns
Assess habitat connectivity to surrounding landscape
Record water-dependent habitat areas

objectives.title: A clear read of existing infrastructure & access

checklist.label: Map all existing roads, tracks, and pathways
Record all existing buildings and structures with condition assessment
Map utility services - water, power, communications, waste
Identify legal access points and access constraints
Record existing fencing and boundary structures
Record current food sources by category - supermarket, local suppliers, own production
Estimate current household waste streams - food waste, packaging, organic material
Map existing household organic outputs available for fertility cycling - kitchen scraps, grey water, humanure potential
Record current energy sources and consumption patterns
Identify all external inputs that could potentially be replaced by home production
Note seasonal patterns in consumption and waste
Inventory all existing food-producing plants - trees, shrubs, perennial vegetables
Assess condition and yield potential of each existing productive element
Identify existing water harvesting or storage infrastructure
Note existing compost, worm farm, or fertility infrastructure
Record any existing animal infrastructure - coops, pens, fencing
Identify areas of existing soil health vs. degradation
Map surrounding land uses within 1km radius
Identify neighbouring agricultural or horticultural practices and their spray, runoff, and contamination risk
Assess prevailing wind direction relative to neighbouring chemical use - drift risk by season
Identify upstream water sources and any contamination risk from surrounding land
Record any landscape-scale pest and weed pressure sources
Note landscape-scale opportunities - foraging, water sharing, community resources

objectives.title: A clear read of how water moves across the site

checklist.label: Map all surface water flows - seasonal and permanent
Identify catchment areas and their contribution to site water
Locate springs, seeps, and water table indicators
Assess runoff patterns and infiltration rates by zone
Map existing drainage infrastructure and its performance

objectives.title: A clear read of soil conditions & subsurface

checklist.label: Conduct soil profile assessment at representative locations across site
Record soil texture, structure, and colour by horizon
Test soil pH, organic matter, and basic nutrient levels
Assess drainage class and water retention characteristics
Map soil type variation across the site
Test all drinking and cooking water sources for biological contamination - bacteria, pathogens
Test for chemical contamination relevant to landscape vector findings - herbicides, nitrates, heavy metals
Assess irrigation water quality for food production suitability
Test rainwater harvesting catchment surfaces for contamination risk
Record seasonal variation in water quality
Define potability status for each water source - drinking, cooking, irrigation, animal use only
Assess total water demand across all enterprises and domestic use
Evaluate water source options - rainfall, groundwater, surface water, municipal
Select primary and backup water supply strategy
Define storage capacity requirements
Select water harvesting approach appropriate to site
Define water conservation priorities and drought response protocol

indicators.metric: Soil moisture by zone
Dam/pond fill level
Domestic supply volume
Stock trough reliability

triggers: Dam below 30% at spring equinox -> restrict irrigation allocation
Domestic supply below household minimum -> review bore yield or augmentation options

checklist.label: Establish zone framework based on use frequency and energy requirements
Define sector influences on zone placement
Allocate enterprise zones based on survey findings
Resolve spatial conflicts between enterprises
Define buffer zones and transition areas
Confirm zone framework against capacity and feasibility constraints

indicators.metric: Zone boundary adherence
Residential boundary conflicts
Kitchen garden zone conditions

triggers: Repeated enterprise encroachment into residential zone -> formalize physical boundary marker
Enterprise crowding kitchen garden -> review zone allocation

checklist.label: Map household food needs from Tier 0 against what the site can produce
Select primary growing methods - annual beds, perennial systems, animals, preserving
Define production targets per food category for first planning cycle
Identify which high-value or high-frequency foods to prioritise first
Define seed saving and variety selection strategy
Establish food preservation and storage approach - fermentation, drying, cellaring, freezing

indicators.metric: Actual harvest by food category vs. planned production targets
Share of household food needs met from own production vs. purchased
Seed-saving success rate and stored seed viability

triggers: Category yield falls well short of target -> review growing method and reallocate beds next cycle
Preserved or stored food spoiling before use -> revisit storage approach and preservation volumes
Saved-seed germination dropping -> refresh varieties or revise seed-saving practice

objectives.title: Define whole-homestead fertility & soil building strategy

checklist.label: Define primary composting approach - hot compost, cold compost, worm farm, bokashi
Map all on-site organic inputs available for composting - kitchen scraps, garden waste, animal manure
Define mulching strategy - sources, materials, application zones
Define animal integration role in fertility cycling if animals are present
Establish cover crop and green manure strategy for garden zones
Define external input reduction targets - what gets replaced, by what, by when

indicators.metric: Compost output volume and finished-compost quality
Soil organic matter and structure in productive zones
Volume of external fertility inputs purchased vs. reduction targets

triggers: Compost failing to heat or break down -> check carbon-to-nitrogen balance and moisture
Soil organic matter not improving in a zone -> increase mulch, cover cropping, or compost application there
External input spend not falling toward target -> trace which loop is still open and prioritise closing it

checklist.label: Assess current shelter condition and adequacy for all seasons
Define heating fuel strategy - wood, passive solar, gas, electric, hybrid
Define backup power strategy - generator, battery, manual alternatives
Identify critical household systems that require power - water pump, refrigeration, communications
Define resilience threshold - minimum energy and shelter standard to maintain in disruption
Identify priority improvements required before Act begins

indicators.metric: Heating fuel reserve level vs. seasonal demand
Indoor temperature and comfort across all rooms during peak cold and heat
Backup power readiness -- fuel, battery charge, last successful test

triggers: Fuel reserve dropping below the season-ahead buffer -> replenish stock and review usage rate
Rooms failing to hold the resilience-threshold temperature -> prioritise insulation or heating upgrade
Backup power test fails -> service or replace the unit before the next disruption window

checklist.label: Design primary vehicle access route from entry to all enterprise zones
Define road and track standards for each use type
Design pedestrian pathways between key working areas
Resolve conflicts between vehicle, animal, and pedestrian movement
Specify turning radii and passing points for farm vehicles

indicators.metric: Track surface condition after wet events
Laneway width adequacy for actual livestock operations
Livestock stress points in the laneway network

triggers: Track surface failure after 25mm+ rain -> repair priority before the next wet event
Livestock stress or bottleneck in a laneway -> review width, corner radius, or surface

objectives.title: A working water harvesting & storage system

checklist.label: Design primary water harvesting structures - dams, swales, tanks, ponds
Specify storage capacity and locations
Design distribution network - pipelines, gravity feeds, pumping systems
Design emergency overflow and spillway infrastructure
Specify materials and construction standards

indicators.metric: Dam/tank fill level as % capacity
Days to refill after a 25mm rainfall event
Trough reliability (recorded empty events)
Domestic supply pressure and volume
Overflow events (date and rainfall trigger)

triggers: Dam not reaching 75% capacity after two 25mm+ events -> investigate inlet, berm, or catchment
Trough empty during operation -> check float valve, supply line, pump
Domestic supply below minimum -> investigate bore, augment storage

checklist.label: Design soil improvement program by zone - composting, mulching, cover cropping
Specify application rates and timing for each zone
Define machinery and equipment requirements
Define priority zones for first-cycle improvement
Establish soil health monitoring baseline for improvement tracking

indicators.metric: Soil organic matter % by zone (lab test)
Bulk density at 0-10cm and 10-20cm
Cover crop establishment rate (% ground cover)
Earthworm counts per square metre

triggers: Bulk density not improving after 18 months in a zone -> investigate mechanical compaction-breaking need
Organic matter not improving in Year 2 -> review input rates and composition
Cover crop failure -> investigate seeding timing, rate, or competition

objectives.title: Design food production zones & garden layout

checklist.label: Design kitchen garden layout - bed dimensions, paths, orientation
Design annual vegetable production zones - rotation blocks, companion planting
Design perennial food zones - orchard, berry areas, perennial vegetables
Design protected growing infrastructure if required - glasshouse, cold frame, tunnel
Design herb and medicinal plant zones
Integrate food production design with fertility system - compost access, mulch application routes
Specify seed saving and nursery area

indicators.metric: Actual yield per production zone vs. the zone area and targets it was designed to deliver
Share of designed beds and growing infrastructure brought into active use
Protected-growing and nursery throughput vs. designed capacity

triggers: A zone consistently under-yielding for its footprint -> revisit bed layout, rotation, or species mix for that zone
Designed beds or growing infrastructure sitting unused -> rephase the build or reassign the space to active production

checklist.label: Design heating system - wood fuel storage, stove placement, passive solar improvements
Specify insulation upgrades required for adequate seasonal performance
Design backup power system - capacity, placement, fuel storage
Design fuel production or storage infrastructure - woodlot, woodshed, fuel security
Specify priority shelter repairs or improvements

indicators.metric: Indoor temperature held by the designed heating system vs. the resilience-threshold standard
Backup power output and run-time vs. the critical-systems load it was sized for
On-site fuel stock and woodlot or woodshed reserve vs. seasonal demand

triggers: Heating system failing to hold the resilience-threshold temperature -> revisit insulation, stove sizing, or passive-solar measures
Backup power unable to carry the designed critical load -> upgrade capacity or shed non-critical demand

checklist.label: Design animal housing - coops, pens, shelters appropriate to species
Design secure runs and grazing areas
Design fencing and predator exclusion
Design feed storage and preparation areas
Design manure management and fertility capture system
Specify water delivery to all animal areas

indicators.metric: Animal housing and run condition vs. species shelter and space standard
Predator-exclusion and fencing integrity -- breaches or losses recorded
Manure captured and returned to fertility loops vs. designed capture volume

triggers: Housing or run condition falling below the species shelter standard -> schedule repair or rebuild before stocking levels rise
Fencing breach or predator loss recorded -> reinforce exclusion at the failure point and recheck the perimeter

checklist.label: Define key indicators to monitor across all enterprises
Design data collection methods and recording systems
Specify monitoring frequency by indicator
Define responsibility for each monitoring stream
Define Observe feedback trigger points - when data initiates a Plan review
Design provision tracking system - record what was grown, preserved, and consumed from the homestead each season
Design gap tracking - record what was still purchased externally and at what cost
Connect tracking system to Tier 0 self-sufficiency targets - measure progress against original baseline
Define seasonal review rhythm - when does the household assess progress and adjust plans
Specify record format - simple enough to maintain consistently
Define Phase 1 scope - which enterprises and infrastructure are included
Sequence Phase 1 tasks in logical implementation order
Assign responsibilities for each Phase 1 task
Define Phase 1 completion milestones
Confirm Phase 1 scope against capacity and resource plan

milestones.metric: Scope creep log -- any addition to Phase 1 scope must be approved and capacity-confirmed

checklist.label: Estimate labour requirements by task and season for Phase 1
Identify skill gaps and training or contractor requirements
Define equipment requirements and sourcing strategy
Estimate Phase 1 capital requirements by category
Define procurement priorities and sourcing plan

milestones.metric: Labour hours deployed vs. estimated by task

checklist.label: Identify top 5-8 risks to Phase 1 implementation
Assess likelihood and impact for each risk
Define contingency response for each risk
Identify early warning indicators for each risk
Assign risk monitoring responsibility

milestones.metric: Early warning indicators monitored as specified per risk
Risk register updated when new risks emerge or status changes

objectives.title: Define provision phasing - quick wins first

checklist.label: Identify fast-yielding enterprises for immediate planting - salad greens, herbs, eggs, sprouts
Sequence medium-term production - annual vegetables, small fruit, preserving crops
Sequence long-term production - orchard, perennial systems, staple crops
Define ecological readiness criteria for each phase - what soil, water, or habitat conditions must be met before each production layer launches
Confirm sequencing reduces household grocery spend from earliest possible point

milestones.metric: Each production layer launched only after its ecological-readiness criteria are met
Household grocery spend reduction vs. provision phasing

objectives.title: Define household budget & input reduction plan

checklist.label: Record current annual household food and supply spend as baseline
Map which spend categories will be reduced in Phase 1 - and by how much
Define input reduction milestones by planning cycle
Identify remaining external inputs that cannot be replaced and their cost
Define minimum financial viability threshold for the homestead

milestones.metric: Input reduction achieved vs. milestones by planning cycle
Household food + supply spend vs. recorded baseline

checklist.label: Define annual homestead review process - what monitoring data is reviewed and by whom
Define decision triggers - what gaps or conditions require a plan change
Specify how plan changes are documented and why
Define 3-year comprehensive review against Tier 0 self-sufficiency targets
Define contingency response for provision shortfalls or system failures - immediate actions when a self-sufficiency target is missed

milestones.metric: Annual homestead review held against monitoring data
3-year comprehensive review against Tier 0 self-sufficiency targets
